capacity_evidence.device: one Blackhole chip from a P300c endpoint (TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0)
capacity_evidence.stage_scope: The optimized batch-one path releases inherited dense expert tensors after loading routed BFP8_B sparse-expert weights and stores persistent K/V in BFP8_B. It removes the functional decoder's quadratic dense causal-mask allocation, accepts caller-selected max_cache_len, extends RoPE tables to that extent, and has no public sequence-alignment or 128-token capacity restriction. The logical context contract therefore returns to the HF-advertised 131072 tokens. Hardware execution in this decoder-only stage qualifies a 256-entry cache through decode position 128; it does not claim an end-to-end 131072-token performance or accuracy run.
capacity_evidence.unprobed_interval: Hardware accuracy and performance above decode position 128 through the HF-advertised 131072-token extent remain unvalidated in this decoder-only stage.

notes: The functional starting point validates real-weight prefill through S=17, and the fused stage validates non-aligned synthetic prefill through S=33. The optimized stage removes the dense-mask design limit and preserves the HF-advertised logical context without introducing a smaller public cap. It preserves non-aligned S=3/17/33 behavior and qualifies its large-prefill program at synthetic S=128. Real-weight S=128 output PCC (layer 12: 0.98922; layer 13: 0.98700) is recorded but is below the selected functional bar, so it is not claimed as real-weight prefill qualification. A complete S=128 prefill plus decode at position 128 validates the extended 256-entry BFP8_B paged cache and layer-aware sliding/full attention boundary behavior. Routine decode positions 3 through 18, repeated paged updates, and trace replay are also validated. Current-token K/V remain BF16 before cache writes. Longer-context hardware qualification is deferred to a later stage; that validation boundary is not exposed as a public context cap.

fused_decoder_stage.capacity_change: No supported-context reduction; current_supported_context advances from 17 to the largest fused prefill validation S=33.

optimized_decoder_stage.validated_decode_positions: 3 through 18 and position 128 after a real-weight S=128 prefill
optimized_decoder_stage.cache_storage_change: BFP8_B replaces BF16 for persistent K/V. For this 1x8x128x64 cache pair, the nominal payload drops from 262144 BF16 bytes to 131072 BFP8 mantissa bytes plus block-exponent metadata.
optimized_decoder_stage.capacity_change: The quadratic dense-mask allocation is removed, so the optimized decoder no longer imposes the earlier device-DRAM context reduction and preserves the HF-advertised 131072-token logical contract. Hardware qualification remains explicitly narrower: synthetic S=128 prefill and real-weight decode at position 128 pass, while real-weight S=128 prefill output remains below the functional PCC bar.

emit_provenance.collective_collapse: Full Hugging Face weights replace emitted shards; attention and expert all-reduces plus routing mesh partition are absent from runtime.

dtype_policy.functional_baseline: BF16 weights, activations, and cache; TILE layout; DRAM by default
dtype_policy.exception: The emitted router promotes its activation and linear result to FP32 before BF16 top-k; decode heads use one workload-derived L1 height shard.
dtype_policy.optimized_policy: BF16 activations, residuals, norms, router, and attention weights; BFP8_B routed expert weights with LoFi sparse matmuls; BFP8_B persistent K/V cache; current-token K/V remain BF16 before cache writes.
dtype_policy.deferred: Complete full-model datatype-frontier selection remains a later datatype-sweep stage.
